chunk_summary: This section discusses integrating ICDS, POSHAN Abhiyaan, and MAA workflows to improve follow-up for feeding difficulties and faltering growth, emphasizing clinic-to-community coordination and synchronized data sharing.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of ICDS in providing supplementary nutrition and growth monitoring for children under 6 years and mothers.
Learn how POSHAN Abhiyaan strengthens growth monitoring through digital tracking and device integration.
Explore strategies for aligning clinic counselling with MAA breastfeeding support to ensure consistent messaging.
Identify key data points for synchronized clinic and community follow-up, including weight, height, and edema status.
key_facts: ICDS supports children under 6 years and mothers with nutrition, growth monitoring, health education, and referrals.
POSHAN Abhiyaan introduced digital tracking to detect growth faltering earlier, improving intervention feasibility.
Families should bring the MCP or RCH growth card to both clinic and anganwadi visits for synchronized data exchange.
Breastfeeding support under MAA prioritizes direct observation and consistent counselling across platforms.
Synchronized weighing intervals reduce family pressure and ensure comparable data for follow-up.
reference_quote: POSHAN Abhiyaan strengthened growth monitoring through devices and digital tracking, improving the feasibility of detecting decline earlier when data are shared. (NITI Aayog, 2019)
document_key_topics: Feeding difficulties and faltering growth follow-up planning
ICDS and POSHAN Abhiyaan integration for growth monitoring
MAA program breastfeeding support and counselling
